post.description:
Creates an access token for the JFrog Platform, or refreshes an existing access token. Only internal Artifactory users can generate a token using basic credentials via REST API.


**Create Token Notes:**
- You can create tokens at the project level if you are a Project Admin.
- You cannot create a token using basic credentials if you enabled multi-factor authentication.
- If you use basic credentials, you must enable creation of tokens in the JFrog Platform UI.


**Refresh Token:**
To refresh an existing access token, set `grant_type` to `refresh_token` and provide the `refresh_token` parameter. The authenticated user must match the user of the access token being refreshed (identified by the refresh token).


**Security:** Requires a valid token or user credentials. Basic authentication is supported when enabled in the platform configuration.

400.description: Invalid input, e.g., `expires_in` value is not a number, non-positive number, etc.

401.description: Unauthenticated

403.description: The requested token details are forbidden, e.g., `expires_in` provided but is higher than the limit defined by the admin, the user does not have the permissions on the scope requested, etc.

get.description:
Returns token information based on the authenticated principal and optional filters. Admin users can view all tokens; non-admin users can only view their own tokens.


**Security:** Requires a valid token.

401.description: Bad Credentials - Invalid credentials

403.description: Permission Denied - Insufficient permissions

get.description:
Returns token details by token ID. You can also use `me` instead of a token ID to get details of the currently used token.


**Note:** Admin users can view any token. Non-admin users can only view tokens where the subject matches their username.


**Security:** Requires a valid token.

401.description: Bad Credentials - Invalid credentials

403.description: Permission Denied - Insufficient permissions

404.description: Not Found - Token not found

delete.description:
Revokes an access token by specifying the token ID. You can also use `me` instead of a token ID to revoke the currently used token.


**Note:** Users can only revoke their own tokens. Admin users can revoke any token.


**Security:** Requires a valid token.

401.description: Bad Credentials - Invalid credentials

403.description: Permission Denied - Insufficient permissions

404.description: Not Found - Token not found

delete.description:
Revokes an access token by providing a JSON web token (JWT) or reference token.


**Note:** The token must be revocable for this operation to succeed.


**Security:** Requires admin privileges.

token.description: The token value to revoke. Can be either a reference token or a JWT access token. The token must have been created with `force_revocable: true` or `refreshable: true` for JWT tokens to be revocable.

revoked_token_id.description: The ID of the revoked token

401.description: Bad Credentials - Invalid credentials

403.description: Permission Denied - Insufficient permissions

404.description: Not Found - Token not found or not revocable

get.description:
Returns the system's token default expiry value.


**Security:** Requires admin privileges.

401.description: Bad Credentials - Invalid credentials

403.description: Permission Denied - Insufficient permissions

put.description:
Sets the system's token default expiry value.


**Security:** Requires admin privileges.

401.description: Bad Credentials - Invalid credentials

post.description:
Creates a pairing token for a specific use case. The pairing token is used to securely establish trust between services.


**Security:** Requires admin privileges.

parameters.name: useCase
parameters.description:
The use case for the pairing token. Supported values:
- `mission-control` - For Mission Control JPD registration
- `bridge` - For Bridge pairing
- `platform-federation` - For Platform Federation

Note: Available use cases depend on your JFrog Platform configuration and enabled services.

401.description: Bad Credentials - Invalid credentials

403.description: Permission Denied - Insufficient permissions

404.description: Not Found - Use case not found or not supported

put.description:
Creates a new permission target in the JFrog Unified Platform or replaces an existing permission target

Since: 2.4.0

Notes: Requires Artifactory Pro. 

- Missing values will be set to the default values as defined by the consumed type.

- Please note when adding Release Bundles permission targets

- Requires an Edge/Enterprise+ license

On Edge nodes, the repositories section in the request body can be left empty or contain the ‘release-bundles’ default repository. No other repositories are allowed. An Enterprise+ license allows you to add any repository

The default repository in the repository section is “release-bundles” for both Edge/EntPlus licenses. (if user create permission target with empty repositories section the repository that we put is “release bundles”)

- To support spaces in the User or Group or Permission names, a Plus symbol (+) is automatically placed when there is a space. This is set by default as the artifactory.

security.api.plus.insteadof.space property is set to true. If you want to use the + (plus) symbol, set the artifactory.security.api.plus.insteadof.space to false.

Security: Requires an admin user

parameters.name: permissionTargetName
parameters.description: The name of the permission target

name.description: The name of the permission target

includesPattern.description: A regex pattern that defines which repositories to include in this permission. The default is "**" (all).

excludesPattern.description: A regex pattern that defines which repositories to explicitly exclude from this permission. The default is "" (none).

repositories.description: Defines the local and remote repositories included as resources for this permission. Note - Virtual repositories are not supported.

users.description: Permissions are set/returned according to the following conventions m=admin; d=delete; w=deploy; n=annotate; r=read; mxm=managedXrayMeta; x=distribute

401.description: Bad Credentials - The provided authentication is invalid.

403.description: Permission Denied - The user does not have the necessary permissions to perform this action.

patch.description:
Configure an existing Artifactory instance using a simple YAML configuration file.
Make single or multiple configuration changes as needed, to create, update, and delete any elements in Artifactory.

**⚠️ Take care when modifying Artifactory configurations:**
Modifying the Artifactory configurations is an advanced feature, and if done incorrectly may render Artifactory
in an undefined and unusable state. Since it is easy to overwrite configurations, such as a repository's package type,
we strongly recommend backing up the configuration before making any direct changes, and taking great care when doing so.

**Note:** This API is available only in Self-hosted instances.

**Security:** Requires an admin user.

**Query parameters are mutually exclusive** - only one operation type can be requested at a time:
- list - Get file/folder listing (flat or deep)
- stats - Get file statistics (download count, last download info)

- properties - Get item properties
- permissions - Get item permissions

get.description:
Returns an artifact from the specified destination.

You can also use properties in deployment and resolution as part of retrieving artifacts.

Special tokens for retrieving latest versions:
- **Latest Maven Release/Integration**: Specify SNAPSHOT or [RELEASE] for the version in the requested path to get the latest Maven integration or release artifact.
- **Latest Non-Maven Release/Integration**: Specify [INTEGRATION] and [RELEASE] for the version in the requested path (replacing [folderItegRev] and [fileItegRev], as defined by the repository's Repository Layouts) to get the latest integration version or latest release version artifact accordingly based on alphabetical sorting.

Notes:
- Integration and release tokens cannot be mixed together.
- Only local, cache and virtual repositories will be used for latest version resolution.
- To change the retrieve latest behavior to retrieve the latest version based on the created date, add `artifactory.request.searchLatestReleaseByDateCreated=true` to `artifactory.system.properties` and restart Artifactory.
- Both [folderItegRev] and [fileItegRev] have to be defined in the repository layout.
- Latest version resolution requires Artifactory Pro.

put.description:
Deploy an artifact to the specified destination by checking if the artifact content already exists in Artifactory.

If Artifactory already contains a user-readable artifact with the same checksum, the artifact content is copied to the new location and returns a response without requiring content transfer.

Otherwise, a 404 error is returned to indicate that content upload is expected to deploy the artifact.

If the X-Checksum-Deploy header is set to false, the artifact will be uploaded successfully with a 201 response, even if it didn't exist before, and submitted checksums will have the status Uploaded: Identical.

You can also attach properties when deploying artifacts.

Since: 2.5.1

parameters.description: Path to the artifact within the repository

parameters.name: X-Checksum-Deploy
parameters.description: Must be set to true for checksum-based deployment

parameters.name: X-Checksum-Sha1
parameters.description: SHA1 checksum value. At least one checksum header (X-Checksum-Sha1, X-Checksum-Sha256, or X-Checksum) must be provided.

parameters.name: X-Checksum-Sha256
parameters.description: SHA256 checksum value. At least one checksum header (X-Checksum-Sha1, X-Checksum-Sha256, or X-Checksum) must be provided.

parameters.name: X-Checksum
parameters.description: Generic checksum value (type resolved by length: 32 chars=MD5, 40 chars=SHA1, 64 chars=SHA256). At least one checksum header (X-Checksum-Sha1, X-Checksum-Sha256, or X-Checksum) must be provided.

put.description:
Deploys an archive containing multiple artifacts and extracts it at the specified destination while maintaining the archive's file structure.

Deployment is performed in a single HTTP request. Only the extracted content is deployed, not the archive file itself.

By default, the deployment of artifacts is performed sequentially. However, starting from Artifactory 7.96.3, artifact deployment can be performed in multiple parallel threads using the system parameter artifactory.explode.archive.threads. For example, if you set artifactory.explode.archive.threads = 10, the deployment of the artifacts contained in the archive will be performed in 10 parallel threads per archive file, which can save considerable time for large archive files.

Supported archive types: zip, tar, tar.gz, tgz

Note: Requires Artifactory Pro. Deployment of compressed archives (unlike tar) may incur considerable CPU overhead.

Since: 2.6.3

post.description:
Zapping cache invalidates all cached metadata artifacts downloaded from central registries like pypi.org, repo1.maven.org, registry.npmjs.org and stored in remote repo cache to speed up remote repo actions. The zapping action does not invalidate immutable artifacts (like software binaries).

After Zapping, whenever an invalidated metadata artifact is needed, Artifactory refreshes it from the central registry first. If unavailable, it falls back to the stale version.

Zapping the cache may slow down for clients who download packages requiring stale metadata updates.

Zapping cache solves:
- Resolving Cache Issues: If there are problems with the cached packages artifacts, such as corruption or inconsistencies with the central repository, zapping the cache fixes these issues.

Since: 7.49.3

post.summary: Post Offline Request Token to JFrog Entitlements Service
post.description:
Posts the offline request token from the air-gapped JPD to the JFrog Entitlements Service and returns the entitlements in JSON format.

You must run this API from a machine that has connectivity to the Internet.

The `offline_response_token` needed for applying entitlements is returned as a response header.

**Example 1 - Save the entitlements to a file:**
```
curl -v -L 'https://jes.jfrog.io/api/v1/offline_register' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --data '{"offline_request_token": "<offline_request_token>"}' \
  > entitlements.json

```
The `-v` flag prints the response headers (including `offline_response_token`) to stderr while saving the response body to `entitlements.json`.

**Example 2 - Extract only the `offline_response_token`:**
```
curl -s -L 'https://jes.jfrog.io/api/v1/offline_register' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --data '{"offline_request_token": "<offline_request_token>"}' \
  -o /dev/null -D - | grep offline_response_token

```
This discards the response body and prints only the `offline_response_token` header value to the screen.

You need **both** the `entitlements.json` file and the `offline_response_token` header value to complete the next step: applying entitlements to the air-gapped JPD using the JFConnect Apply Entitlements API.

**Warning:** If you save the response manually instead of directing the response body to an `entitlements.json` file, ensure that there are no spaces or any modifications. Any change in the response fails checksum validation when you apply entitlements.

offline_response_token.description: The offline response token to be used when applying entitlements to the air-gapped JPD

schema.description: Entitlements data to be saved as entitlements.json and applied to the air-gapped JPD

**JSON mode** (`Content-Type: application/json`): Provide a URL from which Mission Control
will download the encrypted bucket file, along with the decryption key.

**Multipart mode** (`Content-Type: multipart/form-data`): Upload the encrypted bucket file

curl -X POST 'https://<server>/mc/api/v1/buckets' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' \
  -H 'Content-Type: multipart/form-data' \
  -F 'file=@/path/to/bucket-file.json;type=application/octet-stream' \
  -F 'key=<decryption-key>' \
  -F 'name=mybucket'

post.description: Schedules immediate content replication between two Artifactory instances. Replication can optionally include properties and delete items if they do not exist in the source repository. Pull Replication - pulls content from a remote Artifactory repository to a local cache of the remote repository. Push Replication - pushes content from a local repository into a local repository of another Artifactory instance. Multi-push Replication - pushes content from a local repository into a local repository of several Artifactory instances (Enterprise only). The type of replication initiated depends on the type of repository specified in the repoPath parameter. If repoPath is a local repository, a push replication will be triggered. If repoPath is a remote repository cache, a pull replication will be triggered. If no repositories are provided in the payload, Artifactory will trigger all existing replication configurations.

parameters.name: repoPath
parameters.description: Repository path (local repository for push, remote repository cache for pull)

get.summary: Artifact Latest Version Search Based on Layout
get.description:
Search for the latest artifact version by groupId and artifactId, based on the definitions in the Repository Layouts. This API is based on a Maven-specific algorithm for sorting versions. Search can be limited to specific repositories (local, remote-cache, or virtual) by settings the repos parameter. Latest release vs. latest integration Unless the version parameter is specified, the search returns the latest artifact release version. When version is specified, e.g. 1.0-SNAPSHOT, the result is the latest integration version. Remote searches By default only local and cache repositories will be used. When specifying remote=1, Artifactory searches for versions on remote repositories. Filtering results (Artifactory 3.0.2+) The version parameter can accept the * and/or ? wildcards which will then filter the final result to match only those who match the given version pattern. Artifact path pattern The [org] and [module] fields must be specified in the artifact path pattern of the repository layout for this call to work.

Notes: Requires Artifactory Pro

This API is based on a Maven-specific algorithm for sorting versions. 

Results can be filtered by specifying additional properties. 

- {repo} Specify a repository to search through or replace with "_any" to search through all repositories. 
- {path} Specify a path to search through or replace with "_any" to search through all paths. 
- listFiles=0 (default) Artifactory will only retrieve the latest version. 
- listFiles=1 Artifactory will retrieve the latest version and the corresponding files. 

You may specify filters to restrict the set of artifacts that are searched by adding any properties to your search URL.

Notes: Requires Artifactory Pro

Since: 3.1.1

Security: Requires a privileged user (can be anonymous)

post.description:
Triggers a contextual analysis (applicability) scan. This endpoint supports two modes of operation:

**Artifact mode** - scan a specific artifact by providing `repo` and `path` (and optionally `componentId`). The artifact must already be scanned by Xray SCA.

**Aggregate mode** - scan all artifacts in a build or release bundle by providing the corresponding key pair instead:
- For builds: `build_name` + `build_number`
- For Release Bundles V1: `release_bundle_name` + `release_bundle_version`
- For Release Bundles V2: `release_bundle_v2_name` + `release_bundle_v2_version`

The two modes are mutually exclusive. If `repo`, `path`, or `componentId` is provided, the request is treated as artifact mode and aggregate keys are ignored. If none of those are set, the request is treated as aggregate mode.

Optionally, `project` can be provided to scope the request to a specific project.

Requires the MANAGE_DATA permission. Since Xray 3.73.

repo.description: Artifact mode: repository name containing the artifact.

path.description: Artifact mode: path to the artifact in the repository.

project.description: Project key to scope the request (optional, both modes).

release_bundle_name.description: Aggregate mode: Release Bundle V1 name.

release_bundle_version.description: Aggregate mode: Release Bundle V1 version.

release_bundle_v2_name.description: Aggregate mode: Release Bundle V2 name.

release_bundle_v2_version.description: Aggregate mode: Release Bundle V2 version.

get.summary: Search Resources by Vulnerability and Package
get.description:
Search all resources (Artifacts, Builds, Release Bundles, etc.) that include a specific package or are impacted by a specific vulnerability (CVE or XRAY ID). Supports three search modes:

- **Mode 1 - By Vulnerability**: `vulnerability` is required.
- **Mode 2 - By Package Version**: `name` and `type` are required; `version` is optional.
- **Mode 3 - By Package (all versions)**: `name` and `type` are required; omit `version`.

**SBOM Requirement**: This capability depends on the SBOM Service. Self-Hosted users must enable the SBOM feature and complete the SBOM migration. If SBOM is disabled, the API returns 403.

**Pagination**: Use `last_key` from the response to fetch subsequent pages; an empty `last_key` indicates no further results. Limit defaults to 1000, maximum 10000.

Requires the Reports Manager permission. Since Xray 3.131.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of resources to return. Default: 1000. Maximum: 10000.

parameters.name: last_key
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from the previous response. Include this value to retrieve the next page.

parameters.name: vulnerability
parameters.description: Vulnerability ID in CVE format (CVE-YYYY-NNNNN) or XRAY format (XRAY-N). Required for Mode 1.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Package name. Required for Mode 2 and Mode 3.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Package type (e.g., npm, maven, pypi). Required for Mode 2 and Mode 3.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Package version. When provided with name and type, searches for a specific version.

Retrieves the scan results data associated with a specific commit in a branch of a repository. The scan results provide insights into vulnerabilities, SBOM components, operational risks, secrets, IaC issues, and SAST findings detected at the time of the commit.

checkBinaryExistenceAllowed.description: Allows the usage of the checkBinaryExistenceInFilestore flag in the Pull/Push Replication API on the source Artifactory

daysToTrust.description: The number of days for which the full checksum from source Artifactory is trusted. Useful for migration process. The value cannot be more than 30 days. The default is 0.

trustUntil.description: Date until which the trust expires (format varies)

not_match_old.description: Whether the new password must differ from the previous one

VaultConfigRequest.description: Request body when creating, updating, or testing a Vault configuration.

batchSize.description: Optional parameter. The number of records to retrieve for a single DB query, should be an integer greater than 0.

pauseBetweenBatchesMs.description: Optional parameter. This parameter defines the pause (in milliseconds) between successive DB query executions. Should be an integer greater than 0.

batchesCount.description: Optional parameter. This parameter limits the maximum number of DB query executions. For example, if batchesCount = 20 and batchSize is 1,000, then a maximum of (20 * 1,000) = 20,000 records can be deleted

info.description: Information message about the submitted task

admin.description: Whether the user is an admin

effective_admin.description: Whether the user is effectively an admin (based on groups).

profile_updatable.description: Whether the user can update their profile

disable_ui_access.description: Whether UI access is disabled for this user

watch_manager.description: Whether user is a watch manager. Available from Artifactory 7.128.0.

ArtifactsCompareBuildsRequest.description: Two builds to compare (see Compare Builds / build delta in Xray REST API documentation).

source_artifactory_id.description: Name of the first Artifactory instance

target_artifactory_id.description: Name of the second Artifactory instance

ArtifactsBuildVulnerabilityDiffRequest.description: Compares vulnerability differences between two build/component graphs (PDF §12.41).

source_component_id.description: Source component identifier (e.g. build component id).

group_by.description: Grouping key for the diff results.

ArtifactsBuildVulnerabilityDiffResponse.description: Vulnerability diff result grouped by severity or package ID.

data.description: Map of group keys to diff objects containing added, removed, changed, and unchanged vulnerability arrays.

ArtifactsArtifactDependencyGraphRequest.description: Artifact locator for dependency graph (see JFrog documentation). Path is the artifact path (/artifactory-name/repo-name/path); default artifactory name is often 'Artifactory'.

path.description: Full path of the artifact, e.g. /default/my-repo/myArtifact/3.14

XrayGenericJsonRequest.description: JSON request body; refer to JFrog Xray REST API documentation for this operation.

repo.description: Repository name (included in PDF sample alongside path).

CustomIssuesEventMutationRequest.description: JSON body for creating or updating a vendor issue event (Custom Issues API).

id.description: Unique issue identifier. Cannot have an 'Xray' prefix.

provider.description: Vendor or provider identifier. Cannot be 'JFrog'.

severity.description: Issue severity (e.g. High, Medium, Low, Critical).

sources.description: List of sources for this issue.

fixed_versions.description: List of fixed version ranges (null if none).

IgnoreRulesCreateRequest.description: Request body for creating an ignore rule. Must include notes and an ignore_filters object with at least one objective filter.

notes.description: Free-text note explaining why this rule was created.

expires_at.description: Optional expiration date in RFC 3339 format. After this date the rule is marked as expired and stops suppressing violations.

IgnoreRuleFilters.description: Filters that define what to ignore. Combine objective filters (what issue type) with optional scope filters (where to apply). At least one objective filter is required.

vulnerabilities.description: List of vulnerability IDs (XRAY-nnnnn) to ignore. Use ["any"] to ignore all vulnerabilities.

cves.description: List of CVE IDs to ignore. Use ["any"] to ignore all CVEs.

licenses.description: List of license names to ignore. Use ["any"] to ignore all licenses.

operational_risk.description: List of operational risk identifiers. Use ["any"] to ignore all.

scanners.description: List of exposure scanner IDs (e.g., EXP-12345).

categories.description: Exposure categories (secrets, services, applications, iac, malicious_code).

policies.description: Scope filter - list of policy names.

watches.description: Scope filter - list of watch names.

projects.description: Scope filter - list of project keys.

components.description: Scope filter - list of components (name required, version optional).

version.description: Component version. Omit to match all versions.

artifacts.description: Scope filter - list of artifacts (name required, version/path optional).

builds.description: Scope filter - list of builds (name required, version/project optional).

release-bundles.description: Scope filter - list of release bundles (name required, version optional).

release_bundles_v2.description: Scope filter - list of v2 release bundles.

docker-layers.description: Scope filter - list of Docker layer SHA256 hashes.

git_repositories.description: Scope filter - list of git repository names.

IgnoreRuleApiResponse.description: An ignore rule as returned by the GET endpoints.

id.description: Unique external identifier of the ignore rule.

created.description: Timestamp when the rule was created (RFC 3339).

project_key.description: Project key if the rule is project-scoped.

deleted_by.description: User who deleted the rule (if soft-deleted).

deleted_at.description: Timestamp when the rule was deleted (if soft-deleted).

IndexingForceReindexRequest.description: Specifies which artifacts and/or builds to reindex. Provide at least one artifact or build.

artifactory_id.description: Optional Artifactory instance ID. If omitted, the configured instance is used automatically.

project.description: Project key. When set, the build repo is resolved from the project.

is_non_indexed_repo_allowed.description: If true, allows reindexing artifacts from non-indexed repositories.

JiraIntegrationCreateRequest.description: Create Jira integration (PDF §21.1 — JSON body; query parameters none).

connection_name.description: A unique identifier for the connection.

jira_server_url.description: URL of the Jira server where tickets will be created.

skip_proxy.description: Whether to bypass proxy settings. Default false.

JiraIntegrationUpdateRequest.description: Update Jira integration (PDF §21.4 — JSON body; path carries connection name; query parameters none).

connection_name.description: Name of the connection to update.

LegalAssignCustomLicenseRequest.description: Request body for assigning a license to a component.

component.description: Identifies the component to assign the license to.

build_repo.description: Build repository name. Required for build:// components.

license.description: Specifies the license to assign. Must be a known license.

name.description: License identifier. Must be a known license in the system.

full_name.description: Full display name of the license.

references.description: Array of reference URLs for the license.

aliases.description: Array of alternative names for the license.

LegalCreateCustomLicenseRequest.description: Request body for creating a custom license. The name must be unique and cannot exceed 128 characters.

name.description: License key/identifier. Must be unique. Maximum 128 characters.

full_name.description: Full display name of the license.

text.description: Description or full text of the license.

references.description: Array of URLs or references related to the license.

aliases.description: Array of alternative names. Each alias max 128 characters.

category.description: License category. Defaults to Uncategorized if not specified.

PolicyAssignToWatchesRequest.description: Request body containing the list of watch names to assign the policy to.

watches.description: List of watch names to assign the policy to. Each watch is processed independently.

PolicyAssignToWatchesResponse.description: Result of the assignment operation. The `result` object contains one entry per watch name from the request, with a status message indicating the outcome. Possible status values: "Policy assigned successfully to Watch", "Policy was already assigned to Watch", "Watch was not found", "Error Assigning Policy to Watch".

result.description: Map of watch name to assignment status string.

{watch_name}.description: Status message for the watch. Key is the watch name.

PoliciesPolicyMutationRequest.description: Request body for creating or updating a policy. A policy defines rules that determine how Xray identifies and acts on issues found during scanning. Each policy has a type (security, license, or operational_risk) and contains one or more rules. Each rule has criteria (what to match) and actions (what to do when matched).

name.description: Name of the policy. Must be unique across the system (or within a project when using projectKey).

description.description: Optional free-text description of the policy.

type.description: The policy type. Determines which criteria fields are available in the rules.

rules.description: One or more rules that define the policy behavior. Each rule specifies criteria to match and actions to take when matched.

PolicyRule.description: A single rule within a policy. Contains criteria for matching issues and actions to execute when criteria are met.

priority.description: Priority for ordering between rules. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.

PolicyCriteria.description: Criteria that determine which issues trigger the rule. The available fields depend on the policy type. For security policies use min_severity, cvss_range, vulnerability_ids, etc. For license policies use allow_licenses, banned_licenses, etc. For operational_risk policies use op_risk_min_risk, op_risk_custom, etc.

min_severity.description: Minimum severity of vulnerabilities to match. Applies to security and exposures policy types.

vulnerability_ids.description: Match specific CVEs and/or XRAY IDs. A CVE identifier has the format CVE-YYYY-NNNNN, an XRAY ID has the format XRAY-NNNNNN.

fix_version_dependant.description: Only match when a fixed version is available.

applicable_cves_only.description: Only match CVEs determined to be applicable by contextual analysis.

package_name.description: Filter by specific package name. The name format varies between package types.

package_versions.description: Filter by specific package versions. Supports exact versions and ranges. Any version: (,). Specific version: [v1]. Ranges: (v1,v2) open interval, [v1,v2] closed interval, (v1,v2] and [v1,v2) half-open intervals.

allow_licenses.description: List of allowed licenses for license-type policies. Components with licenses not in this list will trigger the rule.

allowed_licenses.description: List of allowed licenses (alias for allow_licenses).

allow_unknown.description: Whether to allow components with unknown licenses.

banned_licenses.description: List of banned licenses. Components with these licenses will trigger the rule.

multi_license_permissive.description: Use permissive approach for components with multiple licenses.

op_risk_min_risk.description: Minimum operational risk level to match.

PolicyCvssRange.description: CVSS score range for filtering vulnerabilities.

PolicyExposures.description: Criteria for matching exposures (contextual analysis findings). Enable specific categories to scope which exposure types trigger the rule.

min_severity.description: Minimum severity of exposures to match.

applications.description: Apply criteria to the applications exposure category.

iac.description: Apply criteria to the Infrastructure as Code (IaC) exposure category.

malicious_code.description: Apply criteria to the malicious code exposure category.

secrets.description: Apply criteria to the secrets exposure category.

services.description: Apply criteria to the services exposure category.

PolicyOpRiskCustom.description: Custom operational risk criteria. Use use_and_condition or use_or_condition to combine multiple conditions.

use_and_condition.description: When true, all custom conditions must be met (AND logic). When false, any single condition triggers the rule (OR logic).

is_eol.description: Match packages that have reached end of life.

commits_less_than.description: Match packages with fewer commits per year than this value.

newer_versions_greater_than.description: Match packages where newer versions exceed this count.

committers_less_than.description: Match packages with fewer committers per year than this value.

release_date_greater_than_months.description: Match packages with a release date older than this many months.

release_cadence_per_year_less_than.description: Match packages with fewer releases per year than this value.

PolicyActions.description: Actions to execute when a rule's criteria are matched. Multiple actions can be combined.

custom_severity.description: Override the severity of the generated violation. Applies only to Operational Risk and License type policies.

webhooks.description: Webhook names to invoke when the rule is triggered.

notify_watch_recipients.description: Notify users subscribed to the watch.

notify_deployer.description: Notify the user who deployed the artifact.

create_ticket_enabled.description: Automatically create a Jira ticket for the violation. Requires a configured Jira integration.

block_release_bundle_distribution.description: Block distribution of release bundles that match the rule.

block_release_bundle_promotion.description: Block promotion of release bundles that match the rule.

fail_build.description: Fail the build if the rule is triggered.

build_failure_grace_period_in_days.description: Grace period in days before the build is failed. Allows time to address the issue without immediately blocking CI/CD.

PolicyBlockDownload.description: Controls whether artifact downloads are blocked.

active.description: Block download of artifacts that match the rule.

unscanned.description: Block download of artifacts that have not been scanned.

grace_period_days.description: Grace period in days before blocking takes effect. Allows time to address the issue.

PolicyApiResponse.description: A policy object as returned by the v1 GET endpoints. Includes read-only fields (author, created, modified) in addition to the fields used for creation.

author.description: The user who created the policy.

rules.description: The rules defined in this policy.

created.description: Timestamp when the policy was created. RFC 3339 format.

modified.description: Timestamp when the policy was last modified. RFC 3339 format.

watches.description: Names of watches this policy is assigned to.

project_key.description: The project key if this is a project-scoped policy. Empty for global policies.

PolicyApiRuleResponse.description: A rule within a policy (v1 response format).

PolicyApiActionsResponse.description: Actions configured for a v1 policy rule. Note: v1 actions do not include block_release_bundle_distribution, block_release_bundle_promotion, create_ticket_enabled, or build_failure_grace_period_in_days — those are available in v2 only.

fail_build.description: Whether builds are failed when this rule triggers.

custom_severity.description: Overridden severity for the generated violation.

PolicyApiV2Response.description: A policy object as returned by the v2 GET endpoints. Includes all v1 fields plus additional v2 action fields.

author.description: The user who created the policy.

rules.description: The rules defined in this policy.

created.description: Timestamp when the policy was created (RFC 3339).

modified.description: Timestamp when the policy was last modified (RFC 3339).

watches.description: Names of watches this policy is assigned to.

project_key.description: The project key if this is a project-scoped policy. Empty for global policies.

PolicyApiV2RuleResponse.description: A rule within a policy (v2 response format).

PolicyActionsV2Response.description: Actions configured for a v2 policy rule. Includes all v1 action fields plus: build_failure_grace_period_in_days, block_release_bundle_distribution, block_release_bundle_promotion, and create_ticket_enabled.

fail_build.description: Whether builds are failed when this rule triggers.

build_failure_grace_period_in_days.description: Grace period in days before the build is failed.

block_release_bundle_distribution.description: Whether distribution of release bundles is blocked.

block_release_bundle_promotion.description: Whether promotion of release bundles is blocked.

custom_severity.description: Overridden severity for the generated violation. Applies only to Operational Risk and License type policies.

create_ticket_enabled.description: Whether a Jira ticket is automatically created. Requires a configured Jira integration.

include_key_patterns.description: Wildcard patterns for including project keys.

exclude_key_patterns.description: Wildcard patterns for excluding project keys.

ReportsResourcesBlock.description: Report scope; provide at least one resource category.

filters.description: Optional filters to narrow the license report results.
Optional filters to narrow the violations report results.

emails.description: Email addresses to notify when the report completes.

cron_schedule.description: Cron expression for scheduling recurring report generation.

cron_schedule_timezone.description: Timezone for the cron schedule (e.g., UTC, America/New_York).

category.description: One of: secrets, services, applications, iac

ReportsGetListRequestBody.description: Optional request body for filtering the reports list. If omitted or empty, all reports are returned (subject to pagination).

filters.description: Filter criteria for the reports list.

name.description: Filter by report name (wildcard patterns supported).

start_time_range.description: Filter by report start time range.

pdf_export_limit.description: Maximum number of rows allowed for PDF export.

max_saved_limit.description: Maximum number of saved reports allowed.

total_reports.description: Total number of reports matching the filters.

report_type.description: Report type: license, vulnerability, operational_risk, violations.

status.description: Report status: completed, running, pending, aborted, failed.

ReportsAbortRequestBody.description: Optional payload when aborting; project scope may use query parameter projectKey.

ReportsVulnerabilityContentRow.description: Vulnerability report row (V2-style; fields vary by release).

ReportsCveSearchReportRequest.description: Request body for generating a CVE Search Report. The CVE and resources scope are required. Filters are optional.

cve.description: The CVE identifier to search for (e.g., CVE-2021-45046).

filters.description: Optional filters to narrow the search results.

ReportsReportContentFiltersBody.description: Optional filters for report table content; pagination is usually via query parameters.

ViolationsSearchRequest.description: Request body for searching violations. Contains a filters object for narrowing results and a pagination object for controlling sort order and paging.

name_contains.description: Filter results where the description contains this string.

include_details.description: Include additional violation detail properties in the response.

min_severity.description: Filter violations with severity equal to or higher than this value.

created_from.description: Filter violations created from this time (RFC 3339).

created_until.description: Filter violations created until this time (RFC 3339).

order_by.description: Sort field. Valid values: created, summary, severity, type, watcher_name, issue_id.

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return. Default: 25.

ArtifactsDependencyGraphNode.description: Recursive dependency node (nested components arrays in responses).

ArtifactsCompareArtifactsRequest.description: Two artifact paths to diff (see Get Artifact Delta in Xray documentation).

ArtifactsArtifactScanStatusRequest.description: Request body for looking up the scan status of a specific artifact.

path.description: Path to the artifact in the repository.

WatchApiV2Request.description: Request body for creating or updating a Watch in Xray.

active.description: Indicates if the Watch is active. Defaults to true if project_resources is included, false otherwise.

project_resources.description: Resources the Watch monitors and their filters.

type.description: Type of the resource to watch.

bin_mgr_id.description: Binary manager ID. Omit or set to default.

repo_type.description: Type of repository (local or remote). Relevant only for repository resources.

build_repo.description: The build or bundle repository. Relevant only for resources under a project.

filters.description: Filters to apply to the resource.

type.description: Filter type. Acceptable types depend on the resource type.

value.description: Filter value. Type depends on filter type: string for regex/path-regex/package-type/mime-type, object with IncludePatterns/ExcludePatterns arrays for ant-patterns/path-ant-patterns, or object with key/value for property.

global_filters.description: Global filters applied across all resources.

watch_recipients.description: Email addresses that will receive Watch notifications.

create_ticket_enabled.description: Indicates whether Jira ticket creation is enabled for this Watch.

ticket_profile.description: Jira profile name for ticket generation.

ticket_generation.description: Ticket generation settings for the Watch.

by_version.description: Settings for creating duplicate tickets by version.

release_bundle.description: Create duplicate tickets for release bundles.

include.description: Impact path to Jira profile mappings.

create_tickets_for_ignored_violation.description: Whether to create Jira tickets for policy violations that have been marked as ignored.

BasicAuth.description: Basic authentication using username and password
